title: MPSC Subordinate Services Group B Combined Pre 2020 (English)

questions.text: _______ started an association by the name 'Granthottejak Mandali' at Nashik.

questions.text: India is the world's largest producer of _______.

a. to meet the needs of health infrastructure and to provide basic reproductive health care.
b. to lower the total fertility rate to the replacement level.
c. to achieve universal immunization of children.

a. Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana was launched during the 11th Five Year Plan.

c. National Food Security Mission was launched in 2009 – 10.

b. to allow the use of ATM and debit cards

d. not to lend in any form

questions.text:
Select the logical column of digits for replacing the column of question marks.
5  4  6  ?
2  6  4  ?
6  4  8  ?
3  5  4  ?

Select the pair of words or word groups that fit in each of the sentences to make two separate and having equivalent meaning in the given context.
The stock market having plunged drastically, the investor's _______ mood on the trading floor seemed incongruous.

Study the accompanying cube with numbers 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6 on its six faces. Number 3 is not opposite to either 1 or 6 and number 6 is not opposite to 4. If this cube is rotated so as to keep number 1 on the top and right side face with number 6 takes the front place that has number 2 now, then which number will be on the right face ?

options.D: More information is needed for selection

questions.text: Accompanying net is folded to get a cube. Select the number of vertices of the cube at which two or more diagonal lines on the faces meet.
Study accompanying transparent sheet of paper X and select the fold that is made using it.

The decline in voter turnout at elections is chiefly explained by the decline in voting by younger people who have very different lifestyles. Older people are much more likely to vote. If this trend continues, probably this will lead to a point at which no government can be said to have a mandate to govern as the number of voters will be too small to indicate the wishes of the majority. Society needs, therefore, to find an alternative to conventional elections so that the wishes of the people can be heard.
Select one option that best expresses the flaw in the above argument.

options.A: Elections are used in all democratic countries as the only well established process of choosing a government.
options.B: With the help of social media, young people may be more likely to join pressure groups than to vote in an election.
options.C: There may be problems in using alternatives such as internet and postal voting due to remote residential places.
options.D: People who are young now may become more likely to vote as they get older.

questions.text:
The following question is based on the information given below :
I. A, B, C, D and E are five persons sitting in a line facing South – while M, N, O, P and Q are five persons sitting in the second line parallel to the first line and facing North, facing each other.
II. E is opposite to O who is just next, right of M.
III. C and N are diagonally opposite to each other.
IV. B who is just next to left of D, is opposite to Q.
V. M is at one end of the line.
VI. P who is just to the left of Q, is opposite to D.
If B shifts to the place of E, E shifts to the place of Q and Q shifts to the place of B, then who will be the second to the left of the person opposite to O ?

In the following question, an Assertion followed by two Reasons RI and RII is given. Apply reasons to the Assertion and find the answer.
Assertion : People, these days, are doing a lot of teleshopping.
Reasons : RI – People are watching TV a lot.
           RII – People, these days, are not finding time to shop in the market.
Answer options :

options.A: Only RI not RII is a possible reason
options.B: Only RII not RI is a possible reason
options.C: Either RI or RII but not both reasons
options.D: Neither RI nor RII is a possible reason

questions.text: Prashant starts from his house and travels 8 km towards North. Then he turns right, travels 5 km, then turns right again and travels 4 km and then travels 3 km towards East. Finally he turns left and travels 2 km. How far is he from his house, in kilometers ?

A survey is conducted on 6200 villagers staying in 4 villages A, B, C and D and having various favourite colours. 50% of people from village B have red as their favourite colour. How many people from other villages have the same favourite colour ?
If the diameter of a circular track is 28 m and there is a 7 m wide road around the track, then find the area of the road.
Five workers take 12 days to weed a field. How many days would 15 workers take to do the same task ?
The sum of the present ages of 3 persons is 72 years. Seven years ago, their ages were in the ratio 4 : 6 : 7. What are their present ages ?
The true discount on a bill due 10 months hence at 6% per annum is ₹ 26.25. The amount of the bill is
A bag contains one-rupee, 50 paise and 25 paise coins in the ratio 5 : 6 : 8. If the total amount is ₹ 420, then the number of 50 paise coins is
American astronaut Christina Koch is the female astronaut spending longest period in space. She spent 328 days in space. On _______, she took off from Earth.

Guinness World Records has taken note of adventure swimmer Prabhat Koli who has won the Central Government Tenzing Norgay National Award and Maharashtra State prestigious Shiv Chhatrapati Bravery Award. So far he has crossed six seas. Among the following, which one water body has he not crossed ?
In the last 72 years after freedom, in our country of 130 crores population, only _______ persons have achieved the honour of 'Param Vir Chakra'.

Select the inappropriate (wrong) option regarding Ayushman Bharat scheme.
a. 10 crore Indian families will be benefited by this scheme.
b. Ayushman card holder family can avail medical treatment upto ₹ 5 lakh free of cost.
c. 30 crore Indian families will be benefited by this scheme.
d. 15 thousand Private Hospitals will get status of Jan Arogya Mitra by this scheme.
Answer options :

    Concept        Utilization
a. UPI          I. Artificial Knowledge Exchange
b. AI           II. Digital payment system
c. GeM          III. Internet of things
d. IoT          IV. Online platform by the Government

b. 2017 – Osmanabad    II. Premanand Gajvi
c. 2018 – Mulund       III. Kirti Shiledar
d. 2019 – Nagpur       IV. Gangaram Gawankar

a. Karnataka ranks first in the use of digital payments.
b. Maharashtra ranks second in the use of digital payments.
c. Bengaluru ranks first in digitised cities.
d. Hyderabad ranks second in digitised cities.
Which of the above sentences are correct ?

Who can participate in the Government of India (MHRD) proposed IMPRINT-II scheme to inculcate culture of innovation among higher education institutes ?
a. Professors in Private Colleges
b. Professors in ITI
c. Professors in IIT, NIT, IIIT
d. Professors in IIT, NIT, IIIT, IISER and Central Universities
Answer options :

For students belonging to the Scheduled Castes and Neo Buddhist class who did not get admission in the Government hostels, to enable them to take post matric education, the Government has started _______ for their lodging, boarding and educational facilities.

options.B: Bharat Ratna Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Swadhar Yojana

questions.text:
For economically backward classes, by the 103rd Constitutional Amendment in 2019, Article 15(6) and 16(6) of the Indian Constitution, following provisions are made. Choose the correct option.
a. This reservation can be availed by persons not benefiting by schemes for SC, ST, OBC.
b. The reservation proportion will be upto 13% for economically backward classes.
c. This reservation is for education and job related benefits.
d. The reservation proportion will be upto 10% for economically backward classes.
Which of the above sentences are correct ?
Answer options :
Prof. Anil Gore, known as 'Marathi Kaka', was awarded State Government _______ award 2019 for popularising Marathi language.
Who is the first Indian to be appointed as General Vice President of International Organisation Development Association (IODA) ?
The Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO) will launch an unmanned campaign in December 2020.
questions.explanation: This question was cancelled by the examination authority.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is correct ?

options.A: On 26th January, 1950, 288 members of the Constituent Assembly signed the Constitution.
options.B: 'Procedure established by Law' was adopted from the Constitution of the United States of America.
options.C: The idea of 'Joint sitting of two Houses of Parliament' was taken from the British Constitution.

questions.text:
Consider the following statements about the 'Preamble' of the Indian Constitution :
a. It states the objects which the Constitution seeks to establish and promote and also aids the legal interpretation of the Constitution where the language is found to be ambiguous.
b. Like any other part of the Constitution, the 'Preamble' was not enacted by the Constituent Assembly.
Answer options :
As observed by Prof. K.C. Wheare, which one of the following statements correctly shows the true nature of the Indian Federal System ?

options.A: The Indian Federation is more a functional than an institutional concept.
options.B: A one dominant party regime which is in conflict with the principles of federalism.
options.C: The Indian federation is not the result of an agreement between the units.
options.D: It is a unitary state with subsidiary federal features rather than a federal state with subsidiary unitary features.

questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
a. It is a joint responsibility of the Sarpanch and Upa Sarpanch to maintain secured custody and proper safety of proceeding register and other relevant records of the Gram Sabha.
b. The first meeting of the Gram Sabha in every financial year shall be held within two months from the commencement of that year.
Answer options :

Consider the following statements :
a. Any resolution of Gram Panchayat shall be modified, amended, varied or cancelled by a Panchayat after a period of three months from the date of the passing thereof by another resolution supported by simple majority.
b. No resolution of Gram Panchayat shall be modified, amended, varied or cancelled by a Panchayat within a period of three months from the date of the passing thereof, except by a resolution supported by two-third of the total number of members of such Panchayat.
Answer options :
The judicial control over administrative actions emanates from the Doctrine of
Which of the following has been wrongly listed as a recommendation of the 'Punchhi' Commission with regard to the Governor ?

options.A: He should have the power to sanction prosecution of a working or retired minister without or against the advice of Council of Ministers.
options.B: He may be removed by a resolution of impeachment passed by the State Legislative Assembly.
options.C: He may be removed by a resolution of impeachment passed by the Rajya Sabha and agreed to by the Lok Sabha.
options.D: Article – 163 of the Constitution should be amended in a way that he may not be able to exercise his discretionary powers in an arbitrary manner.

questions.text: What is Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA) ?

options.A: It is a scheme aimed to enable Panchayat Raj institutions to prepare Gram Panchayat Development plans
options.B: It is a scheme for promoting regular Gram Sabha and their records
options.C: It is a scheme for increasing voting at village level
options.D: It is a scheme for promoting direct election of the Sarpanch

questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
a. Public Accounts Committee has seven members of the Rajya Sabha.
b. It is mentioned in the rules of day-to-day business of Lok Sabha that a member of opposition will be the chairperson of Public Accounts Committee.
Which is/are the incorrect statements from the above ?
